commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Foxes, which were very common, Judges xv. 4. Thus, Horace says: Agros atque lares patrios, habitandaque fana Apris relinquet et edacibus lupis. (Epod. 16.)

commentaries.author: Thomas Aquinas
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Because of the mountain of Zion, which is desolate, the foxes walk upon it. Here is indeed cause for sadness: "For Mount Zion which lies desolate; jackals prowl over it. " That is, where the temple is. And: "jackals prowl over it. " As if they were residing in remote desert places. Thus, the prophet Micah reports: "Therefore because of you Zion shall be plowed as a field; Jerusalem shall become a heap of ruins, and the mountain of the house a wooded height. " (Micah: 3:12).
